notes: Machine-readable bounded recovery policy for HARNESS-018 and HARNESS-029.
This slice is intentionally limited to failure classification, retry eligibility, and escalation decisions.
Retry execution and rollback execution remain later-phase work.

plan_failure.description: The packet or plan is insufficient, contradictory, or missing required constraints.
notes: Plan defects should be revised, not blindly retried.

model_failure.description: The chosen model appears to have produced weak reasoning or repeated misunderstanding.
notes: Prefer a stronger model before escalating when the task remains bounded and evidence is not contradictory.

provider_failure.description: The provider or model endpoint appears unstable, unavailable, rate-limited, or otherwise unable to serve the lane reliably.
notes: Provider failure handling should prefer deterministic routing candidates rather than ad hoc overrides.

tool_failure.description: The tool path or runtime wrapper prevented normal execution or validation.
notes: Do not blame the worker before the tool path is shown to be healthy.

repo_state_failure.description: The repo/worktree/file state made execution unsafe or contradictory.
notes: Repo-state problems require stabilization before retry.

validation_failure.description: Implementation output failed validation or lacked sufficient proof.
notes: Prefer bounded fixes first when the failure is understood and evidence is not contradictory.

ambiguity_failure.description: The requirement itself is too ambiguous to justify a bounded retry.

notes: Same-provider stronger-model retry is only useful when the provider path itself still appears viable.
Provider switch should use approved routing candidates rather than arbitrary overrides.
These conditions stop blind retries and force a human-visible decision path.
